ops.title: cc-present main moves under you after every push (Guides bot auto-commit) — fetch+rebase before advancing the bookmark; gh run list --commit is unreliable here
ops.body:
In cc-present, `main` frequently moves between your commit and your push: the Guides workflow auto-commits "chore: re-render guides" to main after every push, and other sessions land work too. Consequences observed 2026-07-13:

- `ccx vcs ship` fails with `no bookmark to advance (heads(::@- & bookmarks()) matched none)` when main is no longer an ancestor. Recovery: `jj git fetch` → `jj rebase -s <change> -d main` → `jj bookmark move main --to <change>` → `jj git push` (a stale-base bookmark move refuses with "backwards or sideways").
- `gh run list --commit <sha>` often returns empty even when runs exist for that commit; find runs by `displayTitle` prefix (commit subject) or plain `gh run list`, then `gh run watch <id> --exit-status`.
- With parallel lanes (web + ios agents sharing one working copy), land each lane with a path-scoped `jj commit <paths>` so the other lane's in-progress edits stay in `@`.

**Why:** ship's happy path failed twice in one session on these; the recovery steps aren't documented anywhere in the repo.

**How to apply:** before any bookmark advance or ship in this repo, `jj git fetch` first; after any push, expect main to have moved again by the next landing. Related: [[cc-present-dev-daemon-versioning]], [[cc-present-ios-client]].
